data.title: Schlumberger to sell Palliser Block interests
data.description:
Schlumberger (NYSE:SLB) has agreed to sell its interests in the Palliser Block located in Alberta, Canada.

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed. The transaction, which is subject to regulatory approval and other customary closing conditions, is expected to close late in the fourth quarter of 2024.

The Palliser Block consists of oil and gas wells, surface facilities, a pipeline network and certain oil and gas development rights. SLB and Torxen Energy had purchased the Palliser crude oil and natural gas assets from Cenovus Energy in 2017.

The sale announcement comes ahead of SLB’s third quarter results due today. Analysts expect the energy technology company to earn $0.88 (+12.8% Y/Y) on revenue of $9.3B (+11.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Maxim Power repays senior credit facility
data.description:
• Maxim Power (TSX:MXG:CA) announced repayment of amounts owing within the senior credit facility.

  • The corporation has voluntarily repaid the outstanding principal on both the fixed rate construction facility and the bank term facility 1, within the senior credit facility, for a total principal repayment of $49.9 million.

data.title: European indexes mixed after ECB cuts rates
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.27% UK's retail sales rise more than expected in September.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.06% after closing at a fresh record level the day before.

France (CAC:IND) +0.24%

Slovakia posted a current account deficit of €251.3 million in August.

The unemployment rate in Sweden climbed to 8.2% in September.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.11% higher, as traders digest lackluster economic data from China, prospects the ECB will continue to lower interest rates in the coming meetings and fresh corporate results. On Thursday, the ECB cut rates by 25 basis points to 3.25% as expected.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.11%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.22%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.11%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Mitsubishi buys stake in top Philippine fintech platform
data.description:
Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF) will buy a 50% stake in Ayala Corp.’s (OTCPK:AYALY) AC Ventures Holdings for a minimum of P18.4 billion (~$319 million). AC Ventures owns ~13% of Globe Fintech Innovations, (Mynt). Mynt has two fintech companies: G-Xchange- the mobile wallet operator of GCash, and Fuse Lending, a tech-based micro-lender.

This acquisition effectively grants Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF) a 6.5% stake Mynt, the operator of Philippines' biggest fintech platform GCash.

The partnership aims to enhance Mynt's growth both domestically and internationally, particularly in areas like cloud-based payments and new credit solutions.

The deal was disclosed by Ayala (OTCPK:AYALY) on October 18, 2024, and is subject to regulatory approvals.

The collaboration is seen as a renewal of a long-standing partnership that dates back to 1974, which has previously included projects across various sectors such as renewable energy and auto dealerships.

“We believe Mitsubishi can add meaningful value to Mynt, which will allow Mynt to deliver significant value to its over 94 million registered users. It’s all about serving better the many Filipinos that depend on GCash and Fuse, and for making a wider variety of financial and other products available to as many Filipinos as possible,” said Cezar Consing, President and CEO of Ayala (OTCPK:AYALY).

data.title: Toppan Holdings acquires HID's Citizen Identity Solutions business
data.description:
• Toppan Holdings (OTCPK:TOPPY) to acquire HID's CID (Citizen Identity Solutions) business area, a division of HID Global Corporation focused on the government identity sector.
  • At the forefront of secure government identity solutions, HID's CID business comes with over 20 years of experience, serving more than 50 countries worldwide.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BNP Paribas, Prudential gets nod to set up insurance units in China - report
data.description:
China approved the establishment of two new insurance units, BNP Paribas (OTCQX:BNPQF) and Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU), as a part of the country's ongoing efforts to liberalize its insurance market and attract foreign investment, Reuters reported on Friday.

The government has also given a nod to BNP Paribas (OTCQX:BNPQY) and Volkswagen Financial Services, an arm of the Volkswagen Group (OTCPK:VWAGY), to form a joint company, Li Yunze, the head of China's National Financial Regulatory Administration, said at a financial forum in Beijing. This joint venture aims to offer insurance, which is one of the fastest-growing financial sectors in China.

Prudential Financial (PRU) has also received authorization to launch an insurance asset management company in Beijing. "Next, we will support Beijing's financial opening up as always," Li commented, according to the report.

This aligns with broader regulatory reforms aimed at integrating international firms into China's financial landscape.

data.title: Sphere 3D mines fewer Bitcoin in September vs year ago
data.description:
• Sphere 3D Corp (NASDAQ:ANY) mined 9.2 Bitcoin in September, compared to 63.6 Bitcoin mined in the year ago period.
  • The decrease reflects significant changes at one colocation site, where the hosting provider bought out the company's contract, Sphere 3D Corp (ANY) said.
  • "The company is currently evaluating miners from this site and is actively exploring more affordable hosting options."
  • Month-end deployed hash rate was 0.7 EH/s.
  • Development initiated on new 12.5 MW hosting site with multi-year sub $0.04 power contract; energization begins in December 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: UK's retail sales rise more than expected in September
data.description:
Retail Sales in the United Kingdom increased 0.30% in September over the previous month, following a rise of 1.0% in August.

The figure beats the consensus of 0.3% fall. 

Retail sales rose 3.9% Y/Y, more than estimates of 3.2%.

ETFs: (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB).

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: Cencora secures $2B modification contract for U.S. defense logistics
data.description:
• AmerisourceBergen Drug, now known as Cencora (NYSE:COR) awarded an estimated ~$2B modification extending the third two-year six-month option period of a two-year six-month base contract with three two-year six-month option periods for prime vendor pharmaceutical and pharmaceutical-related products and support.
  • This is a firm-fixed-price, requirements contract.
  • Type of appropriation is fiscal 2025 through 2026 defense working capital funds.
  • The contracting activity is the Defense Logistics Agency, Pennsylvania.
